comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma G2.** Prove rigorously the alternating formula
$$c_{(a,1^b)}(n)=\sum_{i=0}^b(-1)^i\#\{w\in\OSP(n,n-b+i)\ :\ \inv(w)=a+i\}\qquad(\star)$$
by equating the Rhoades–Wilson series (Thm `\ref{thm:hilbert}`) with the super-Schur expansion (Prop. `\ref{prop:ds}`).

**Key point requiring care.** Using the hook super-Schur identity $s_{(a,1^b)}(q/u)=q^au^b+q^{a-1}u^{b+1}$, the LHS becomes
$$1+\sum_{a\ge1,\,b\ge0}c_{(a,1^b)}(n)\,(q^au^b+q^{a-1}u^{b+1}).$$
Each monomial $q^au^b$ receives contributions from **two distinct hooks**: from $(a,1^b)$ (its $q^au^b$ term) and from $(a+1,1^{b-1})$ (its $q^{(a+1)-1}u^{(b-1)+1}=q^au^b$ term). The proof must show:

- (i) no other hook contributes to $q^au^b$;
- (ii) the RHS $u$-degree bookkeeping $u^{n-d}$ pairs with $b=n-d$, i.e. the relevant stratum is $\OSP(n,n-b)$;
- (iii) boundary case $b=0$: only $(a)$ contributes, giving $c_{(a)}(n)=\#\{w\in\OSP(n,n):\inv(w)=a\}$.

This yields the recurrence
$$c_{(a,1^b)}(n)+c_{(a+1,1^{b-1})}(n)=\#\{w\in\OSP(n,n-b):\inv(w)=a\}\quad(b\ge1),$$
which telescopes/solves to $(\star)$. Provide the explicit solution of the recurrence (the telescoping must be written out).

**Status:** verified numerically for $n\le6$. The gap is rigor of the extraction and the telescoping solution.
